steps.prompt: Which word is a verb meaning 'we praise'?

hints.Te: 'Te' is a pronoun in the accusative meaning 'you'
hints.Deum: 'Deum' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'God'
hints.Dominum: 'Dominum' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'Lord'

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive 1st pl. of 'laudare' would be 'laudemus' — 'laudamus' has the indicative -a- stem
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect 1st pl. of 'laudare' would be 'laudavimus' — 'laudamus' is present tense
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect 1st pl. of 'laudare' would be 'laudabamus' — 'laudamus' is present tense

hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amare' (to love)
hints.cano, canere, cecini, cantum: These are the principal parts of 'canere' (to sing)
hints.adoro, adorare, adoravi, adoratum: These are the principal parts of 'adorare' (to adore/worship)

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive 1st pl. would be 'confiteamur' — 'confitemur' is present indicative
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect 1st pl. would be 'confessi sumus' — 'confitemur' is present tense
hints.Future indicative: Future 1st pl. would be 'confitebimur' — 'confitemur' is present tense

hints.confido, confidere, confisus sum: These are the principal parts of 'confidere' (to trust)
hints.confirmo, confirmare, confirmavi, confir: These are the principal parts of 'confirmare' (to confirm)
hints.fateor, fateri, fassus sum: These are the principal parts of 'fateri' (to confess) — 'confitemur' is the compound 'confiteri'

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'Deus' is 'Deus' — 'Deum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Deus' is 'Dei' — 'Deum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'Deus' would be 'Deo' — 'Deum' has the -um accusative ending

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'Dei' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Dominus' — 'Dominum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'Dominus' would be 'Domino' — 'Dominum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Dominus' would be 'Domine' — 'Dominum' has the -um accusative ending

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'Domini' ends in -i

translation: We praise you, God; we acknowledge you, Lord
